title: geum urbanum subvar. hirtum
intro: Geum urbanum subvar. hirtum, also known as hairy wood avens, is a variant of the perennial herb Geum urbanum, native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Geum urbanum subvar. hirtum can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, reduce skin irritations, and promote overall well-being.
